basics.name: Felipe I. Gutiérrez González
basics.label: PhD Researcher
basics.summary: PhD candidate in Engineering Sciences at PUC, currently a Visiting Research Intern at the AIDOS Lab, University of Fribourg. Affiliated PhD Researcher at the National Center for Artificial Intelligence (CENIA).

work.name: AIDOS Lab, University of Fribourg
work.summary: Conducted international research collaboration focusing on the intersection of Deep Learning and Topological Data Analysis (TDA). Investigated topology-based measures of generalization of deep neural networks based on training trajectories.

work.name: Pontificia Universidad Católica de Chile
work.summary: Project: 'Neural Bases of Language Acquisition'. Processed and analyzed EEG data from infants. Conducted research into Temporal Response Function (TRF) models.

work.name: Banco de Crédito e Inversiones (BCI)
work.summary: Developed a novel system to detect model decalibration by implementing a 'Topological Uncertainty' score. Conducted benchmarking study on vector databases.

work.name: Entel
work.summary: Designed and trained a deep learning model for Speech Emotion Recognition from audio recordings.

work.name: Entel
work.summary: Prototyped and evaluated RSS-based algorithms for user geolocation using mobile network antenna signal strength.

work.name: MiDaS UC
work.summary: Optimized wastewater sampling strategies for COVID-19 surveillance by processing geographical and graph data.

work.name: ISA Intervial
work.summary: Implemented a Bayesian statistical model to quantify and predict vehicular accident risk across a national highway network.

work.name: Economatica
work.summary: Automated institutional data pipelines by developing web data retrieval and processing projects.

work.name: AES Gener
work.summary: Analyzed geographic data to select optimal sites for residential-scale renewable energy projects.

awards.title: National Doctorate Scholarship
awards.awarder: National Research and Development Agency (ANID), Chile

awards.title: Top-Ranked Graduate (Rank 1/12)
awards.awarder: Master in Data Management and Processing, PUC

publications.name: Model Transferability Informed by Embedding's Topology
Identifying outbreaks in sewer networks: An adaptive sampling scheme under network's uncertainty
publications.publisher: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

publications.name: SpatialCluster: A Python library for urban clustering
Learning to cluster urban areas: two competitive approaches and an empirical validation
